title: Install advisor — Reporter on Log10x SaaS (most basic)
description: Simplest install path: user picks the standalone Reporter DaemonSet (zero-touch, runs alongside any existing forwarder) and sends TenXSummary metrics to the Log10x SaaS backend (no external TSDB credentials). With backends=['log10x'] there is no backend_credentials question, and the Reporter shape has no forwarder question (forwarder selection is Receiver-only) — so license acquisition is the only remaining structural decision (auto-minted via signin_required → log10x_signin_start, or pasted via license_source='paste'). Canonical chain: log10x_discover_env (snapshot k8s; skip_aws=true in CI) → log10x_advise_install (app=reporter, backends=['log10x']). Test enforces (1) discover_env runs and emits a snapshot id, (2) advise_install is called with that snapshot id, app=reporter, and 'log10x' in backends — across any of its calls in the conversation, (3) advise_install's last response is a structurally valid wizard outcome (plan envelope, signin_required, or a next_question asking only for license).
prompt: Install the Log10x Reporter on this cluster. Just send the engine metrics to the Log10x SaaS backend — I don't need to wire up Datadog or any other TSDB.

initial_tool.reason: Install chains start at discover_env. skip_aws=true keeps the eval-harness probe local-only on the demo env (we're not credentialed for AWS in CI).

ground_truth.description: advise_install was called with a snapshot_id arg referencing a disc- id (chain handoff from discover_env)
advise_install reached a structurally valid wizard outcome — plan envelope (Install plan / Verify / helm) OR a typed wizard mode (signin_required / next_question / license_paste)
